analysis_result: BCAT_BILD_ET_AL_UP in MPNST-G1: NES = -1.17, FDR q-val = 0.551
discrepancy_analysis:
A. REPORTED vs MODEL
   - Reported (Supp Fig 6a, MPNST-G1 panel): FDR = 0.4132, NES = 1.0129 (positive)
   - Model: FDR = 0.551, NES = -1.17 (negative — sign-flipped)

B. CONCORDANCE ASSESSMENT
   - NES: |1.0129 - (-1.17)| / |1.0129| = 216% relative difference + sign flip. MAJOR discrepancy.
   - FDR: |0.4132 - 0.551| / 0.4132 = 33% relative difference. MAJOR discrepancy.
   - Both metrics exceed the ±10% concordance band; additionally the NES sign is inverted.

C. ROOT-CAUSE INVESTIGATION
   Paper evidence:
   - Supp Fig 6 is split into two panels: panel a shows enrichment plots for MPNST-G1 Tumors (WNT_UP, BCAT_BILD_ET_AL, CYCLIN_D1_KE, CYCLIN_D1_UP with positive NES ≈ 1.0-1.3 and all non-significant FDRs ≈ 0.22-0.44), panel b shows the same four gene sets in MPNST-G2 with larger positive NES (1.58-1.94) and significant FDRs.
   - In Figure 3c (main text), BCAT_BILD_ET_AL_UP appears in the MPNST-G2 top-10 upregulated list (positive MPNST-G2 NES), consistent with Supp Fig 6b.
   - Both panels of Supp Fig 6 show positive NES for the same gene set, which is logically impossible from a single two-class GSEA comparison (G1 vs G2). In a paired G1-vs-G2 contrast NES would be equal in magnitude but opposite in sign between the two phenotype orientations.
   - This indicates the authors did NOT perform a single G1-vs-G2 comparison; they ran two separate single-sample/one-group enrichment analyses (e.g., each subgroup vs. the rest of the cohort, or each subgroup vs. neurofibroma baseline, or ssGSEA-style internal rankings), producing independent NES values for each panel.

   Model approach:
   - Classic two-phenotype GSEA: 13 MPNST samples split G1 (n=6) vs G2 (n=7), Welch t-statistic ranking, G1 as phenotype of interest, 10,000 permutations, seed 42.
   - Produced NES = -1.17 (gene set depleted in G1 / enriched in G2), which is consistent with Figure 3c and Supp Fig 6b but NOT with the specific Supp Fig 6a value.

D. ROOT CAUSE CLASSIFICATION
   5:STATISTICAL_METHOD — the model reproduced a plausible G1-vs-G2 two-class GSEA, but Supp Fig 6 was generated with a different GSEA design (per-group one-sided enrichment, not a paired G1-vs-G2 contrast). Under the method implied by the paper's own layout of Supp Fig 6 (separate panels with independent positive NES), both groups can have positive NES for the same gene set — something a two-class design cannot produce.

E. CONFIDENCE IN ROOT CAUSE
   HIGH that the root cause is methodological (study design of the GSEA run), because the panel layout itself proves the authors used a design different from the model's. The exact alternative design (ssGSEA, per-subgroup-vs-rest, per-subgroup-vs-neurofibroma) is not stated in the caption, so the specific fix is uncertain, but the class of error is clear.

F. MAGNITUDE
   MAJOR — both NES and FDR are >20% different, and NES sign is flipped.

G. PROPOSED FIX
   Re-run GSEA using one of the alternative designs that can produce independent positive NES per subgroup:
   (i) ssGSEA / single-sample GSEA with the BCAT_BILD_ET_AL_UP signature, then compare mean scores in G1 alone; or
   (ii) GSEA G1 vs non-G1 (pooling G2 + neurofibromas) rather than G1 vs G2; or
   (iii) GSEA G1 vs neurofibroma baseline only.
   Given Supp Fig 6 compares within MPNSTs only, option (ii) with non-G1 = G2 is closest to a two-class design, but that would still give symmetric NES. Most likely the authors used ssGSEA or a one-sided rank-based enrichment per subgroup (consistent with both panels showing independent positive NES).
proposed_fix: Replace the two-class G1-vs-G2 GSEA with the per-subgroup enrichment design implied by the Supp Fig 6 panel layout (independent positive NES in each of panels a and b). Most likely ssGSEA/single-sample scoring of the BCAT_BILD_ET_AL_UP signature within G1 samples, or a G1-vs-rest (G2 + neurofibromas) contrast — not a paired G1-vs-G2 comparison. Confirm with the authors which specific GSEA design produced the Supp Fig 6 panels.
